warning: AI-assisted monitoring. Verify against official legislation before product approval, export, label declaration, or customer compliance confirmation.

rules.sourceTitle: Standard for Food Additive Use - GB2760-2015
rules.notes: This standard specifies the varieties, scope of use, and maximum usage levels of food additives.
rules.evidenceQuote: Standard for Food Additive Use - GB2760-2015

rules.foodCategory: All foods (including seasonings and condiments, snack foods)
rules.limit: Verification required via the Codex General Standard for Food Additives (GSFA) Online Database (Codex STAN 192-1995)
rules.sourceTitle: Food Additives DB (GSFA Online) | CODEXALIMENTARIUS FAO-WHO
rules.notes: The GSFA sets forth the conditions under which permitted food additives may be used in all foods. Users are encouraged to consult the database and its Preamble for specific provisions.
rules.evidenceQuote: The 'Codex General Standard for Food Additives' (GSFA, Codex STAN 192-1995) sets forth the conditions under which permitted food additives may be used in all foods...

rules.foodCategory: All foods (including seasonings and condiments, snack foods)
rules.parameter: Contaminants (e.g., mycotoxins, metals like arsenic, lead, mercury)
rules.limit: Verification required via the General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995)
rules.sourceTitle: Contaminants | CODEXALIMENTARIUS FAO-WHO
rules.notes: Codex establishes maximum levels (MLs) for contaminants to protect human health. The Codex Committee on Contaminants in Food (CCCF) establishes and endorses these levels.
rules.evidenceQuote: The Codex maximum level (ML) for a contaminant in a food or feed commodity is the maximum concentration of that substance recommended by the Codex Alimentarius Commission to be legally permitted in that commodity.

rules.limit: Follow the Code of Practice for the Prevention and Reduction of Mycotoxins in Spices (CXC 78-2017)
rules.sourceTitle: Contaminants | CODEXALIMENTARIUS FAO-WHO
rules.notes: This Code of Practice provides guidance for preventing and reducing mycotoxin contamination in spices.
rules.evidenceQuote: CXC 78-2017 Code of Practice for the Prevention and Reduction of Mycotoxins in Spices

rules.sourceTitle: Database - Food Safety - European Commission
rules.notes: The Additives Database provides information on food additives approved for use in the EU and their conditions of use, based on the Union list of food additives in Annex II of Regulation (EC) No 1333/2008.
rules.evidenceQuote: This database can serve as a tool to inform about the food additives approved for use in food in the EU and their conditions of use. It is based on the Union list of food additives. This list is in Annex II of Regulation (EC) No 1333/2008.

rules.parameter: Foodborne bacteria, toxins, and metabolites (e.g., Salmonella, Listeria monocytogenes, Enterobacter sakazakii, staphylococcal enterotoxins, histamine)
rules.sourceTitle: Microbiological criteria - Food Safety - European Commission
rules.notes: Commission Regulation (EC) No 2073/2005 lays down food safety criteria for relevant foodborne bacteria, their toxins and metabolites in specific foods, and also process hygiene criteria.
rules.evidenceQuote: Commission Regulation (EC) No 2073/2005 on microbiological criteria for foods, applicable from 1 January 2006, lays down food safety criteria for relevant foodborne bacteria, their toxins and metabolites, such as Salmonella , Listeria monocytogenes , Enterobacter sakazakii , staphylococcal enterotoxins and histamine in specific foods.

rules.foodCategory: Prepackaged food stuffs (including seasoning, snack food, processed foods)
rules.sourceTitle: GSO 9:2022 - Labeling of prepackaged food stuffs
rules.notes: This GSO technical regulation specifies general requirements for the labelling of prepackaged foodstuffs.
rules.evidenceQuote: GSO 9:2022 لائحة فنية خليجية --> بطاقات المواد الغذائية المعبأة

rules.foodCategory: Food products (including seasoning, snack food, processed foods)
rules.sourceTitle: GSO 2055-1:2015 - HALAL FOOD - Part 1 : General Requirements
rules.notes: This GSO technical regulation outlines general requirements for Halal food.
rules.evidenceQuote: GSO 2055-1:2015 لائحة فنية خليجية --> الأغذية الحلال – الجزء الأول : الاشتراطات العامة للأغذية الحلال

rules.foodCategory: Food products (including seasoning, snack food, processed foods)
rules.sourceTitle: GSO 150-1:2013 - Expiration dates for food products - Part 1 : Mandatory expiration dates
rules.notes: This GSO technical regulation specifies mandatory expiration dates for food products.
rules.evidenceQuote: GSO 150-1:2013 لائحة فنية خليجية --> فترات صلاحية المنتجات الغذائية - الجزء الأول : فترات الصلاحية الإلزامية

rules.foodCategory: Food products (including seasoning, snack food, processed foods)
rules.sourceTitle: GSO 150-2:2013 - Expiration dates for food products - Part 2 : Voluntary expiration dates
rules.notes: This GSO standard provides guidelines for voluntary expiration dates for food products.
rules.evidenceQuote: GSO 150-2:2013 مواصفة قياسية خليجية --> فترات صلاحية المنتجات الغذائية - الجزء الثاني : فترات الصلاحية الاختيارية

rules.foodCategory: Foodstuffs intended for human consumption, food ingredients (including seasoning, snack food, processed foods)
rules.sourceTitle: GSO 1016:2015 - Microbiological Criteria for Foodstuffs
rules.notes: This GSO technical regulation is concerned with microbiological limits for various foodstuffs and food ingredients used in the food industry.
rules.evidenceQuote: This GSO technical regulation is concerned with microbiological limits for some foodstuffs intended for human consumption and for some food ingredients used in food industry.

rules.sourceTitle: Regulation of the Indonesian Food and Drug Authority Number 15 of 2020 on the Amendment to the Regulation of the Indonesian Food and Drug Authority Control Number 30 Of 2017 on the Surveillance of Drugs and Food Imported to the Territory of Indonesia
rules.notes: Imported processed foods are subject to surveillance to ensure compliance with Indonesian additive limits.
rules.evidenceQuote: Surveillance of Drugs and Food Imported to the Territory of Indonesia

rules.sourceTitle: Surat Edaran Nomor 2 Tahun 2026 tentang Ketentuan Produksi, Importasi, Registrasi, dan Peredaran Bahan Tambahan Pangan Dinitrogen Monoksida (N2O)
rules.notes: Specific regulations exist for the production, import, registration, and circulation of Dinitrogen Monoxide (N2O) as a food additive.
rules.evidenceQuote: Ketentuan Produksi, Importasi, Registrasi, dan Peredaran Bahan Tambahan Pangan Dinitrogen Monoksida (N2O)

rules.sourceTitle: Regulation of the Indonesian Food and Drug Authority Number 15 of 2020 on the Amendment to the Regulation of the Indonesian Food and Drug Authority Control Number 30 Of 2017 on the Surveillance of Drugs and Food Imported to the Territory of Indonesia
rules.notes: Imported processed foods are subject to surveillance to ensure compliance with Indonesian contaminant limits.
rules.evidenceQuote: Surveillance of Drugs and Food Imported to the Territory of Indonesia

rules.sourceTitle: Regulation of the Indonesian Food and Drug Authority Number 15 of 2020 on the Amendment to the Regulation of the Indonesian Food and Drug Authority Control Number 30 Of 2017 on the Surveillance of Drugs and Food Imported to the Territory of Indonesia
rules.notes: Imported processed foods are subject to surveillance to ensure compliance with Indonesian labelling requirements.
rules.evidenceQuote: Surveillance of Drugs and Food Imported to the Territory of Indonesia

rules.sourceTitle: Peraturan Badan Pengawas Obat dan Makanan Nomor 6 Tahun 2026 tentang Tata Cara Penerbitan Surat Keterangan Ekspor Pangan Olahan
rules.notes: This regulation outlines the procedures for obtaining an export certificate for processed foods, implying that such a certificate is required for export.
rules.evidenceQuote: Tata Cara Penerbitan Surat Keterangan Ekspor Pangan Olahan

rules.sourceTitle: Peraturan Kepala BPOM Nomor 34 Tahun 2019 Tentang Kategori Pangan
rules.notes: This regulation establishes the classification system for food categories, which is essential for correct product identification and labelling of processed foods.
rules.evidenceQuote: Kategori Pangan

rules.sourceTitle: Regulation of the Indonesian Food and Drug Authority Number 15 of 2020 on the Amendment to the Regulation of the Indonesian Food and Drug Authority Control Number 30 Of 2017 on the Surveillance of Drugs and Food Imported to the Territory of Indonesia
rules.notes: Imported processed foods are subject to surveillance to ensure compliance with Indonesian microbiology limits.
rules.evidenceQuote: Surveillance of Drugs and Food Imported to the Territory of Indonesia

rules.limit: Verification required (prohibited unless specified by MHLW as harmless)
rules.sourceTitle: Food Sanitation Act
rules.notes: Excludes natural flavoring agents and generally consumed substances used as additives. MHLW specifies which additives are unlikely to cause harm.
rules.evidenceQuote: Additives (excluding natural flavoring agents and things that have generally been served for human consumption, and are used as additives) and preparations and food containing additives must not be sold... except for cases that the Minister of Health, Labour and Welfare specifies as unlikely to cause harm to human health

rules.parameter: Contamination or addition of foreign substances in food/additives
rules.limit: Prohibited for sale/handling if likely to cause harm
rules.sourceTitle: Food Sanitation Act
rules.notes: Applies to food or additives likely to cause harm to human health due to contamination or the addition of foreign substances or on other grounds.
rules.evidenceQuote: those which are likely to cause harm to human health due to contamination or the addition of foreign substances or on other grounds.

rules.parameter: Residues of agricultural chemicals, feed additives, pharmaceuticals
rules.limit: Prohibited if exceeding MHLW specified quantity
rules.sourceTitle: Food Sanitation Act
rules.notes: MHLW specifies quantities unlikely to cause harm. This is the basis for the Positive List System.
rules.evidenceQuote: Food in which substances that are the ingredients of agricultural chemicals... feed additives... and pharmaceutical products... remain in a quantity exceeding the quantity that the Minister of Health, Labour and Welfare specifies as unlikely to cause harm to human health... must not be produced, imported, processed, used, cooked, preserved, for the purpose of sale, or sold

rules.parameter: Standards for apparatus, containers, packaging, raw materials, and production methods
rules.limit: Verification required (must conform to MHLW established standards/criteria)
rules.sourceTitle: Food Sanitation Act
rules.notes: The Minister of Health, Labour and Welfare may establish standards for apparatus, or containers and packaging, or their raw materials, and criteria for production methods. Non-conforming items are prohibited.
rules.evidenceQuote: The Minister of Health, Labour and Welfare may establish standards for apparatus, or containers and packaging, or the raw materials thereof... or may establish the criteria for the production methods thereof... the apparatus, or containers and packaging that does not conform to the standards must not be sold, produced or imported for the purpose of sale, or used in business

rules.parameter: Toxic or harmful substances in apparatus, containers, and packaging
rules.limit: Prohibited for sale/production/import/use if likely to cause harm
rules.sourceTitle: Food Sanitation Act
rules.notes: Also applies if they have a harmful effect on food or additives.
rules.evidenceQuote: Apparatus, or containers and packaging which contains or bears toxic or harmful substances and is likely to cause harm to human health, or apparatus, or containers and packaging which comes into contact with food or additives and has a harmful effect on them and which is likely to cause harm to human health must not be sold, produced or imported for the purpose of sale, or used in business.

rules.parameter: Toxic or harmful substances in food/additives
rules.sourceTitle: Food Sanitation Act
rules.notes: Exception when the Minister of Health, Labour and Welfare specifies that the substances are unlikely to cause harm to human health.
rules.evidenceQuote: those which contain or bear toxic or harmful substances or are suspected to contain or bear such substances; provided, however, that this does not apply when the Minister of Health, Labour and Welfare specifies that the substances are unlikely to cause harm to human health

rules.sourceTitle: Food Sanitation Act
rules.notes: Food business operators must endeavor to ensure the safety of food, additives, apparatus, or containers and packaging.
rules.evidenceQuote: A food business operator... must endeavor to ensure the safety of the food, additives, apparatus, or containers and packaging

rules.limit: Take necessary measures appropriately and promptly
rules.sourceTitle: Food Sanitation Act
rules.notes: Includes providing records to authorities and disposing of hazardous food.
rules.evidenceQuote: A food business operator must endeavor to take any necessary measures appropriately and promptly, such as the provision of the record prescribed in the preceding paragraph to the national government or prefectures, etc. and the disposal of the food for sale, etc. which caused the food sanitation hazards.

rules.parameter: Import of food/additives requiring sanitation management certification
rules.limit: Must have certificates from exporting country's governmental organization
rules.sourceTitle: Food Sanitation Act
rules.notes: Applies to food and additives specified by Ministry of Health, Labour and Welfare Order.
rules.evidenceQuote: The food and additives specified by Ministry of Health, Labour and Welfare Order as requiring certification of the conditions of food sanitation management at the production site... must not be imported for the purpose of sale, unless they have certificates or copies thereof attached which have been issued by a governmental organization of an exporting country and which contain the relevant matters.

rules.parameter: Import of food/additives requiring special process control
rules.limit: Must be produced/processed in MHLW specified countries/facilities
rules.sourceTitle: Food Sanitation Act
rules.notes: Applies to food and additives specified by Ministry of Health, Labour and Welfare Order.
rules.evidenceQuote: The food and additives specified by Ministry of Health, Labour and Welfare Order as requiring measures to control particularly important processes... must not be imported for the purpose of sale, unless they have been produced or processed in the countries or regions, or facilities specified by the Minister of Health, Labour and Welfare as actually taking those measures.

rules.parameter: Production, processing, use, cooking, preservation methods, and ingredients
rules.limit: Verification required (must conform to MHLW established criteria/standards)
rules.sourceTitle: Food Sanitation Act
rules.notes: The Minister of Health, Labour and Welfare may establish criteria for methods and standards for ingredients. Non-conforming food or additives are prohibited.
rules.evidenceQuote: The Minister of Health, Labour and Welfare may establish the criteria for the methods of producing, processing, using, cooking, or preserving food or additives... or may establish standards for the ingredients of food or additives... food or additives that do not conform to such standards must not be produced, imported, processed, used, cooked, preserved, or sold.

rules.limit: Endeavor to make and retain records
rules.sourceTitle: Food Sanitation Act
rules.notes: Records include information such as the name of the person who sold food or raw materials.
rules.evidenceQuote: A food business operator must endeavor to make a record of any necessary information such as the name of the person who has sold food for sale, etc. or raw materials thereof to the food business operator and retain the record

rules.sourceTitle: Food Sanitation Act
rules.notes: Applies to collection, production, processing, use, cooking, storage, transport, display, and delivery of food or additives.
rules.evidenceQuote: Food or additives which are meant to be sold... must be collected, produced, processed, used, cooked, stored, transported, displayed and delivered in a clean and sanitary manner.

rules.parameter: Cleanliness of apparatus, containers, and packaging
rules.sourceTitle: Food Sanitation Act
rules.notes: Applies to apparatus and containers and packaging used in business.
rules.evidenceQuote: Apparatus, and containers and packaging used in conducting business must be clean and sanitary.

rules.parameter: Import of livestock meat, milk, organs, and products thereof
rules.limit: Must have certificates from exporting country's governmental organization
rules.sourceTitle: Food Sanitation Act
rules.notes: Certificates must confirm absence of specified diseases/abnormalities. Exception for countries with telecommunications data exchange.
rules.evidenceQuote: The meat, milk and organs of livestock... must not be imported as food for the purpose of sale, unless they have certificates or their copies attached which have been issued by a governmental organization of an exporting country, and which certify that they are not the meat, milk or organs of livestock... which have or are suspected to have any of the diseases listed in each item of the preceding paragraph

rules.limit: Prohibited for sale/handling if likely to cause harm
rules.sourceTitle: Food Sanitation Act
rules.notes: Applies to food or additives contaminated with pathogens or suspected to be so, and which are likely to cause harm to human health.
rules.evidenceQuote: those which are contaminated with pathogens or are suspected to be so, and which are likely to cause harm to human health

rules.sourceTitle: Food Sanitation Act
rules.notes: Exception for those unlikely to cause harm to human health and recognized as edible.
rules.evidenceQuote: those which are rotten or have deteriorated, or are immature; provided, however, that this does not apply to those which are unlikely to cause harm to human health in general and are recognized as edible

rules.parameter: General labelling requirements for prepackaged foodstuffs, including allergen declaration and nutritional information (e.g., salt/sodium)
rules.sourceTitle: GSO 9:2022 لائحة فنية خليجية - بطاقات المواد الغذائية المعبأة
rules.notes: This technical regulation specifies the mandatory information that must appear on labels of prepackaged food products in GCC countries. This is expected to cover allergen declarations and nutritional facts, which would include salt/sodium content.
rules.evidenceQuote: GSO 9:2022 لائحة فنية خليجية --> بطاقات المواد الغذائية المعبأة

rules.sourceTitle: GSO 150-1:2013 لائحة فنية خليجية - فترات صلاحية المنتجات الغذائية - الجزء الأول : فترات الصلاحية الإلزامية
rules.notes: This technical regulation specifies the mandatory shelf life requirements for food products in GCC countries, which must be declared on the label.
rules.evidenceQuote: GSO 150-1:2013 لائحة فنية خليجية --> فترات صلاحية المنتجات الغذائية - الجزء الأول : فترات الصلاحية الإلزامية

rules.limit: Must be assessed for safety by SFA
rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Food Additives
rules.evidenceQuote: Food additives must be assessed for safety by SFA before they can be used in food sold in Singapore.

rules.limit: Only permitted additives allowed; verification required
rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Food Additives
rules.notes: Verification required using SFA's List of Permitted Food Additives or Food Additives Search tool.
rules.evidenceQuote: Only food additives that have been assessed by SFA are permitted for use in food products sold in Singapore.

rules.limit: Compliance with specific regulatory limits required
rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Food Additives
rules.notes: Limits vary depending on the additive and food type. Refer to 'Guidance Information on Requirements for Use of Food Additives' and SFA's Legislation.
rules.evidenceQuote: Different limits apply, depending on the additive and the type of food products.

rules.parameter: Multiple Class II chemical preservatives (e.g., sorbic acid, benzoic acid)
rules.limit: Calculation tool required for permitted levels
rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Food Additives
rules.notes: Use the 'Calculation Tool for Class II Chemical Preservatives' when two or more are used.
rules.evidenceQuote: If two or more Class II chemical preservatives are to be used in the food (e.g. sorbic acid or benzoic acid), use the Calculation Tool for Class II Chemical Preservatives to calculate the permitted levels that should be used.

rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Food Additives
rules.notes: An application can be submitted to SFA to evaluate the use of a non-permitted additive.
rules.evidenceQuote: If your food product contains an additive that is not permitted, do not import or sell it.

rules.limit: Must be within SFA regulatory limits
rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.notes: Specific limits for various contaminants (microbiological, heavy metals, mycotoxins, veterinary drug residues, pesticide residues, other incidental constituents) require verification from SFA's relevant documents and Legislation.
rules.evidenceQuote: Contaminants (also known as incidental constituents) in food sold in Singapore must be within SFA’s regulatory limits.

rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.evidenceQuote: If your food or food products contain contaminants or incidental constituents that exceed the regulatory limits, do not import or sell them.

rules.limit: Compliance with established Maximum Residue Limits (MRLs)
rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.notes: MRLs can be downloaded from the SFA website and will be included in future amendments of the Food Regulations.
rules.evidenceQuote: SFA has also assessed the risk of the presence of other veterinary drug residues in various food commodities, and has established a list of maximum residue limits (MRLs) for veterinary drug residues for compliance by food businesses with effect from 1 May 2020.

rules.limit: Must be declared in list of ingredients on food labels
rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Food Additives
rules.evidenceQuote: All ingredients and additives used in food products must be declared in list of ingredients on food labels.

rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Food Additives
rules.evidenceQuote: Your food or food product must also comply with the labelling requirements.

rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.notes: This is an exemption from testing, not a limit itself.
rules.evidenceQuote: Ready-to-eat (RTE) food are not required to be tested for total plate count (TPC).

rules.sourceTitle: Use Restrictions and Labeling Requirements of γ-Aminobutyric Acid Produced by Microbial Fermentation as a Food Ingredient
rules.notes: Specific use restrictions and labeling requirements must be verified in the referenced regulation.
rules.evidenceQuote: Use Restrictions and Labeling Requirements of γ-Aminobutyric Acid Produced by Microbial Fermentation as a Food Ingredient

rules.parameter: Cephalopods and cephalopod products, Spiral shells and Spiral shell products thereof, Seeds and seed products (excluding highly refined sunflower seed oil), Kiwis and kiwi products thereof
rules.sourceTitle: Regulations Governing Food Allergen Labeling on the Recommended Labeling Allergens
rules.notes: Labeling is recommended if these allergens are present as an ingredient or food additive.
rules.evidenceQuote: food allergen labeling is also recommended for the products that contain any of the following allergens as an ingredient or food additive: (1) Cephalopods and cephalopod products... (2) Spiral shells and Spiral shell products thereof... (3) Seeds and seed products... (4) Kiwis and kiwi products thereof...

rules.parameter: Crustacean shellfish, mango, peanuts, sesame seeds, milk and goat milk, eggs, tree nuts, gluten-containing cereals, soybeans, fish, sulphite
rules.sourceTitle: Regulations Governing Food Allergen Labeling on the Recommended Labeling Allergens
rules.notes: Labeling is required if these allergens are present as an ingredient or food additive. The limit of 10 mg/kg applies to sulphite as residue sulphur dioxide.
rules.evidenceQuote: For prepackaged foods, food allergen labeling is required for the products that contain any of the following 11 allergens as an ingredient or food additive... and sulphite as the residue sulphur dioxide for 10 or more milligrams per kilogram of food.

rules.sourceTitle: Standards for Pesticide Residue Limits in Foods
rules.notes: Specific limits must be verified in the referenced standard.
rules.evidenceQuote: Standards for Pesticide Residue Limits in Foods

rules.sourceTitle: Standards for Pesticide Residue Limits in Animal Products
rules.notes: Specific limits must be verified in the referenced standard.
rules.evidenceQuote: Standards for Pesticide Residue Limits in Animal Products

rules.sourceTitle: Regulations Governing Food Allergen Labeling on the Recommended Labeling Allergens
rules.notes: A precautionary allergen labeling statement is suggested if there is a risk of allergen cross-contamination.
rules.evidenceQuote: If there is a risk of a food product being affected by allergen cross-contamination... the following statement of precautionary allergen labeling is suggested: “ The plant, equipment or production line used to produce the product is also used to process○○” or the synonymous terms.

rules.sourceTitle: Revised Regulations on Nutrition Claim for Prepackaged Food Products
rules.notes: Specific requirements for nutrition claims must be verified in the referenced regulation.
rules.evidenceQuote: Revised Regulations on Nutrition Claim for Prepackaged Food Products

rules.foodCategory: seasoning powder, seasoning sauce, snack seasoning, ready-to-eat snacks
rules.sourceTitle: กองอาหาร (Food Division)
rules.notes: Specific limits and permitted uses for food additives must be verified against current regulations.
rules.evidenceQuote: วัตถุเจือปนอาหาร

rules.foodCategory: seasoning powder, seasoning sauce, snack seasoning, ready-to-eat snacks
rules.sourceTitle: กองอาหาร (Food Division)
rules.notes: Specific limits for contaminants must be verified against current food standards.
rules.evidenceQuote: มาตรฐานอาหารที่มีสารปนเปื้อน

rules.foodCategory: seasoning powder, seasoning sauce, snack seasoning, ready-to-eat snacks
rules.sourceTitle: กองอาหาร (Food Division)
rules.notes: Food products must comply with the maximum residue limits for toxic substances as specified in the updated ASEAN MRLs list.
rules.evidenceQuote: บัญชีค่าปริมาณสารพิษตกค้างสูงสุดของอาเซียน (ASEAN MRLs) (ฉบับปรับปรุง กุมภาพันธ์ 2569)

rules.foodCategory: seasoning powder, seasoning sauce, snack seasoning, ready-to-eat snacks
rules.parameter: Toxic Substances and Veterinary Drug Residues
rules.sourceTitle: กองอาหาร (Food Division)
rules.notes: Specific limits for toxic substances and veterinary drug residues must be verified against current food standards.
rules.evidenceQuote: มาตรฐานอาหารที่มีสารพิษ-ยาสัตว์ตกค้าง

rules.foodCategory: seasoning powder, seasoning sauce, snack seasoning, ready-to-eat snacks
rules.sourceTitle: กองอาหาร (Food Division)
rules.notes: All food products must comply with general food labeling and nutrition labeling requirements as per relevant regulations.
rules.evidenceQuote: การแสดงฉลากอาหารและฉลากโภชนาการ

rules.foodCategory: seasoning powder, seasoning sauce, snack seasoning, ready-to-eat snacks
rules.sourceTitle: กองอาหาร (Food Division)
rules.notes: A list of foods prohibited from production, import, or sale exists and must be verified.
rules.evidenceQuote: อาหารที่ห้ามผลิต นำเข้า หรือจำหน่าย

rules.foodCategory: seasoning powder, seasoning sauce, snack seasoning, ready-to-eat snacks
rules.sourceTitle: กองอาหาร (Food Division)
rules.notes: Specific limits for pathogenic microorganisms must be verified against current food standards.
rules.evidenceQuote: มาตรฐานอาหารด้านจุลินทรีย์ที่ทำให้เกิดโรค

rules.sourceTitle: Substances Added to Food (formerly EAFUS) | FDA
rules.notes: Delisted color additives are listed in FDA regulations (21 CFR 81.10 and/or 81.30).
rules.evidenceQuote: Delisted color additives in FDA regulations (21 CFR 81.10 and/or 81.30)

rules.sourceTitle: Substances Added to Food (formerly EAFUS) | FDA
rules.notes: Food additives and color additives must be listed in FDA regulations (21 CFR Parts 172, 173 and Parts 73, 74 respectively).
rules.evidenceQuote: Food additives and color additives that are listed in FDA regulations (21 CFR Parts 172, 173 and Parts 73, 74 respectively)

rules.sourceTitle: Substances Added to Food (formerly EAFUS) | FDA
rules.notes: GRAS substances must be listed in FDA regulations (21 CFR Parts 182 and 184).
rules.evidenceQuote: Generally Recognized as Safe ("GRAS") substances that are listed in FDA regulations (21 CFR Parts 182 and 184).

rules.sourceTitle: Substances Added to Food (formerly EAFUS) | FDA
rules.notes: Substances approved for specific uses in foods prior to September 6, 1958, known as prior-sanctioned substances (21 CFR Part 181).
rules.evidenceQuote: Substances approved for specific uses in foods prior to September 6, 1958, known as prior-sanctioned substances (21 CFR Part 181).

rules.sourceTitle: Substances Added to Food (formerly EAFUS) | FDA
rules.notes: Prohibited substances are listed in FDA regulations (21 CFR Part 189).
rules.evidenceQuote: Prohibited substances that are listed in FDA regulations (21 CFR Part 189) as prohibited from use in food

rules.limit: Milk, eggs, fish, Crustacean shellfish, tree nuts, peanuts, wheat, soybeans, and sesame
rules.sourceTitle: Food Allergies | FDA
rules.notes: These 9 allergens are identified by the Food Allergen Labeling and Consumer Protection Act of 2004 (FALCPA) and the FASTER Act. The effective date is for sesame.
rules.evidenceQuote: The major food allergens are milk, eggs, fish, Crustacean shellfish, tree nuts, peanuts, wheat, soybeans, and sesame.

rules.sourceTitle: Chemical Contaminants Transparency Tool
rules.notes: This is a Guidance Level.
rules.evidenceQuote: 3-Monochloropropane-1,2-Diol (3-MCPD) Acid-hydrolyzed protein and Asian-style sauces Guidance Level 1 ppm

rules.sourceTitle: Chemical Contaminants Transparency Tool
rules.notes: This is an Action Level.
rules.evidenceQuote: Aflatoxins, total Foods Action Level 20 ppb

rules.sourceTitle: Chemical Contaminants Transparency Tool
rules.notes: This is an Action Level.
rules.evidenceQuote: Aflatoxins, total Peanuts and peanut products Action Level 20 ppb

rules.sourceTitle: Chemical Contaminants Transparency Tool
rules.notes: Contaminant levels include tolerances, action levels, guidance levels, derived intervention levels, recommended maximum levels, and advisory levels. Tolerances and action levels are defined in 21 CFR parts 109 and 509.
rules.evidenceQuote: The FDA has published a variety of contaminant levels for poisonous or deleterious substances in human food in various guidance documents and regulations for industry.

rules.limit: Must meet FDA's defined standard for gluten content
rules.sourceTitle: Food Allergies | FDA
rules.notes: Specific compliance requirements exist for fermented and hydrolyzed foods bearing the "gluten-free" claim (effective August 12, 2020).
rules.evidenceQuote: On August 2, 2013, the FDA issued a final rule defining “gluten-free” for food labeling... On August 12, 2020, the FDA issued a final rule to establish compliance requirements for fermented and hydrolyzed foods... bearing the “gluten-free” claim.

rules.limit: Not required by law; should not substitute good manufacturing practices
rules.sourceTitle: Food Allergies | FDA
rules.notes: Such statements are used to address unavoidable "cross-contact" only if manufacturers have incorporated good manufacturing processes.
rules.evidenceQuote: Such statements are not required by law and can be used to address unavoidable “cross-contact,” only if manufacturers have incorporated good manufacturing processes in their facility

rules.sourceTitle: Food Allergies | FDA
rules.notes: The "Current Good Manufacturing Practice, Hazard Analysis, and Risk-Based Preventive Controls for Human Food" rule (CGMP & PC rule) establishes requirements for allergen preventive controls to prevent cross-contact and undeclared allergens.
rules.evidenceQuote: The CGMP & PC rule includes requirements for allergen preventive controls to prevent allergen cross-contact in manufacturing and packaging and to prevent undeclared allergens.

rules.limit: Must be declared on the label
rules.sourceTitle: Food Allergies | FDA
rules.notes: Identified as allergenic substances derived from insects.
rules.evidenceQuote: Color additives made from cochineal extract and carmine... have been identified as allergenic substances that must be declared on the label of all food and cosmetic products.

rules.sourceTitle: Food Allergies | FDA
rules.notes: Applies to most packaged food products.
rules.evidenceQuote: FALCPA requires that foods or ingredients that contain a "major food allergen" be specifically labeled with the name of the allergen source.

rules.parameter: Declaration of specific types of tree nuts, fish, and Crustacean shellfish
rules.limit: Type of tree nut, species of fish, and species of Crustacean shellfish must be declared
rules.sourceTitle: Food Allergies | FDA
rules.notes: Examples: almonds, pecans, walnuts for tree nuts; bass, flounder, cod for fish; crab, lobster, shrimp for Crustacean shellfish.
rules.evidenceQuote: The law also requires that the type of tree nut (for example, almonds, pecans, walnuts), and the species of fish (for example, bass, flounder, cod) and Crustacean shellfish (for example, crab, lobster, shrimp) to be declared.

rules.sourceTitle: Food Allergies | FDA
rules.notes: Required due to potential hypersensitivity reactions in some individuals.
rules.evidenceQuote: The FDA requires all products containing FD&C Yellow No. 5 to identify it on their labels

rules.parameter: Format for Major Food Allergen Declaration
rules.limit: In parentheses after ingredient name OR in a "contains" statement
rules.sourceTitle: Food Allergies | FDA
rules.notes: Examples: “lecithin (soy),” “flour (wheat),” “whey (milk)” or “Contains wheat, milk, and soy.”
rules.evidenceQuote: The name of the food source of a major food allergen must appear: In parentheses following the name of the ingredient. — OR — Immediately after or next to the list of ingredients in a “contains” statement.

rules.sourceTitle: Food Allergies | FDA
rules.notes: Must be declared on food labels when present at or above the specified concentration.
rules.evidenceQuote: Various sulfiting agents... must be declared on food labels when present in food and the concentration in the food is ≥10 parts per million total sulfites.

rules.sourceTitle: Hệ thống văn bản Chính phủ
rules.notes: Specific regulations regarding food additive limits for seasoning, snack food, and processed foods must be searched and verified within the official government legal document database, particularly those issued by the Ministry of Health.
rules.evidenceQuote: Lĩnh vực: ... Y tế - Sức khỏe ... Cơ quan ban hành: ... Bộ Y tế
